questions.question: How does backpropagation compute gradients?
questions.reference_answer: Backpropagation computes gradients by applying the chain rule from the output layer backward through the network.

questions.question: Why are convolutional neural networks parameter-efficient?
questions.reference_answer: CNNs use shared weights and local receptive fields, which reduces the number of learnable parameters.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of self-attention in Transformers?
questions.reference_answer: Self-attention lets the model weigh relationships across all positions in the sequence and capture long-range dependencies.

questions.question: What does a vector store do in a RAG pipeline?
questions.reference_answer: A vector store indexes embeddings and retrieves the most similar chunks for a query.

questions.question: What is the role of the chunker in RAG?
questions.reference_answer: The chunker splits documents into overlapping text segments so they can be embedded and retrieved efficiently.
